reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures the informal claim. The hypothesis `‖z‖ = 1` on `z : ℂ` matches "any point of the unit circle" (complex norm equals |z|). The sequence `s n = ∑ i ∈ range n, i * z^i` is the standard partial sum $\sum_{i=0}^{n-1} i z^i$ of the power series $\sum n z^n$ (the $i=0$ term vanishes, matching the usual convention). The conclusion `¬ ∃ y, Tendsto s atTop (𝓝 y)` is the standard formalization of "the series does not converge" — i.e., the sequence of partial sums has no limit. The universal quantification over `z` via the theorem parameter with hypothesis `hz` correctly encodes "on any point of the unit circle". The extra hypothesis `h : s = ...` merely defines `s`, which is logically equivalent to inlining.
